summary:
The appropriate level of remaining mercury post-restoration should maintain structural integrity; studies recommend at least 50% but ideally between 58-62%.

The summary accurately captures Agent2’s focus on achieving balance through an adequately high yet specific range of residual mercury essential for successful amalgam durability while adhering strictly to two concise statements as requested. ```plaintext
To summarize Agent2's reasoning into just two sentences focusing only on critical clinical aspects related to the question asked regarding optimum residual mercury levels in amalgam restorations:

Optimal amalgam function requires retaining significant mercury—ideally above 50%, preferably ranging from 58%-62%—to guarantee bond strength and resistance against degradation over extended periods.
```
